etymology.description: semantic 氵(氵) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 難(難) (nán) -> meaning "beach; shore"
etymology.interpretation: The character 灘 (tān) combines the semantic component 氵(氵) (shuǐ, "water") to indicate an association with the sea or river, with the phonetic component 難(難) (nán) providing the pronunciation. Together, they suggest a landform related to water, specifically a beach or shore.
etymology.simplification: Simplified from 灘 by replacing 難(難) (nán) with 难(难) for phonetic preservation while reducing strokes.

examples.chinese: 我们在海滩上散步(我們在海灘上散步)。
examples.english: We are taking a walk on the beach.

examples.chinese: 盛夏时分(盛夏時分)，人们喜欢去海滩游泳(人們喜歡去海灘游泳)。
examples.pinyin: Shèng xià shí fēn, rénmen xǐhuān qù hǎitān yóuyǒng.
examples.english: In midsummer, people like to go to the beach to swim.

examples.chinese: 沙滩上的贝壳很漂亮(沙灘上的貝殼很漂亮)。
examples.pinyin: Shātān shàng de bèiké hěn piàoliang.
examples.english: The shells on the sandy shore are beautiful.
